name: create_pr
description:
Opens a pull request in a GitHub repository. Always returns a preview first — call with confirmed=false (default) to review, then confirmed=true to execute. Requires GitHub connector token with 'repo' scope.
Use when: user wants to open a pull request to merge a branch into a target branch.
Scope: remote GitHub API — write operation, requires token.
Not for: creating issues (use manage_issue), pushing commits (use local git), or reviewing existing PRs (use get_repo_info).

head.description: The branch containing your changes. Example: 'feature/my-feature' or 'username:branch' for forks.

base.description: The target branch to merge into. Default: repo's default branch (usually 'main').

draft.description: Open as a draft PR. Default: false.

confirmed.description: Must be true to execute the write. When false (default), returns a preview without making any changes. Always review the preview before setting confirmed=true.
